items.q: What does it mean for an allele to be 'dominant'?
items.correct: It is expressed in the phenotype when at least one copy is present (in a heterozygote).
items.misconception: A dominant allele is the most common allele in a population.
items.random_wrong: A dominant allele physically destroys the recessive allele in the genome.
items.why: The everyday meaning of 'dominant' (most prevalent, strongest) misleads students into thinking it relates to frequency or power.

items.q: How do decomposers return carbon to the atmosphere?
items.correct: They respire — breaking down dead organic matter and releasing CO2.
items.misconception: Decomposers release carbon by physically breaking matter into smaller pieces.
items.random_wrong: Decomposers turn dead matter into oxygen and water, with no carbon involved.
items.why: Students see decomposition as a physical breakdown rather than a metabolic respiration process happening in microbes and fungi.

items.q: How are signals transmitted across a synapse between two neurones?
items.correct: Via neurotransmitter chemicals released from the presynaptic neurone, which bind receptors on the postsynaptic neurone.
items.misconception: The electrical signal jumps directly from one neurone to the next across the synaptic gap.
items.random_wrong: Neurones touch each other at synapses and pass current through direct contact.
items.why: Students think of nerves as 'wires' carrying electricity straight through, missing the chemical step at each synapse.
Correct answer: Natural selection. “Lamarckism” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Golden rice is GM to produce a precursor of:
items.why: Correct answer: Vitamin A. “Vitamin C” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Reforestation absorbs roughly how many tonnes CO₂ per hectare per year?
items.why: The real figure is 3. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
